Text: Sparq Internal Use Only ShipExec / ConnectShip installation and configuration Note: All steps below should be followed in order, and if a section is unable to be completed, please do not continue and get assistance from the team. This is especially important on a customer’s server. ConnectShip credentials ● (If you are installing on a customer’s server and upgrading from an older ShipExec 1.x to 2.x, they will already have ConnectShip credentials, which you’ll need to request. For your own local install, use the steps below.) ● You will need to to register with ConnectShip to receive a custom login, and be included on the UPS License and access its Carriers, Services, Printers and other settings. This is important for installing ConnectShip later on in this guide. ● Use this ConnectShip URl to register for Member Access, to have your credentials created: https://connectship.com/contact/?ref=register. During registration, on the line for the ConnectShip License ID, use CH89725. That ID was created for Sparq and our local setups only. ● Usually the next day, ConnectShip will email you your new user ID and initial password. Go to the main ConnectShip home, then to the Member home page and log in with the temporary credentials, then change your password ● Review the License and Password information on the right hand side of the page. Show the password and copy the credentials. That will be the license and password used in the ConnectShip PMC licensing configuration later on. Install Chrome ● Download and install Chrome if it has not been already. ● UPS highly recommends Chrome to be installed and used with ShipExec 2, but MS Edge is also acceptable if a customer requires using it instead. Install .Net Framework 4.8 ● .Net 4.8 is required for ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip, and might already be installed depending on the windows or server version. You can confirm the version installed by these steps: ○ Run regedit.exe, either from the Windows > Run box or a command prompt. ○ Open the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Microsoft\NET Framework Setup\NDP\v4\Full folder. ○ Note the Version value in the right pane. If it’s below 4.8, download and install that version in the following steps: ■ Download and install and take defaults during the setup: https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet-framework/net48 ■ Note: For installing on a customer’s server, select the .Net Framework 4.8 Runtime. If installing for your local setup, select the Developer Pack. Install IIS and other features ● For a local install on Windows 10, open Control Panel then Programs and Features. On the left select Turn Windows features on or off. Make sure at least the following are selected, and use the images below as a full guide. Note the gray checkmarks aren’t disabled items, it’s just the current view. Those items should be installed. ● For installing on a customer’s server, open the Server Roles and Features. Sparq internal use only Below are screenshots for the minimum Roles and Features required for ShipExec 2.x, found on Windows Server 2012 and newer, to use as a
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: just the current view. Those items should be installed. ● For installing on a customer’s server, open the Server Roles and Features. Sparq internal use only Below are screenshots for the minimum Roles and Features required for ShipExec 2.x, found on Windows Server 2012 and newer, to use as a guide. Similar selections exist for a local setup on Windows 10. You can select more options if needed for your particular setup. (The screenshots may indicate .Net 4.7, even if 4.8 is installed) Note: If installing ShipExec 2.x on a customer’s server where an older ShipExec already exists, leave any current selections checked to support the older product. To avoid errors with the server being unable to locate source media to install features, it’s recommended to select features under the Web Server section, install them, then open the wizard again to add other features below it and restart as needed. Sparq internal use only Notes: ● ASP is legacy but still required to install ConnectShip (PMC), if installing on a new server. ● IIS 6 Management Compatibility isn’t required for ShipExec 2.x and can be unchecked, only if installing on a new server. If the server has an existing ShipExec 1.x setup, leave the option checked. Sparq internal use only Install SQL and Tools ● (If installing on a customer’s server and they already have SQL Server, skip this step) ● For a local setup, you can install MS SQL Express Server 2019. (Or you can download SQL Developer edition for a SQL server with less limitations and more options): ○ https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=101064 ● If installing on a customer’s server, SQL Express will not be sufficient - the 10 GB max database size limit will cause issues with PMC/ConnectShip. The customer will need to provide a SQL server instance to use that fits their business requirements. ● Install SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS). This is not required but very convenient for working either locally or on a customer’s shipexec server to access the DB, instead of having to remote into their external SQL server. ○ You can typically install the latest version of SSMS. ○ Note: If installing on older Server 2012, it’s recommended to download an older version of SSMS such as 2016, since newer versions install Azure Data services which is a large part of the install and unnecessary for ShipExec. Older SSMS versions can be found on the download site under Previous Versions. ○ https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/ssms/download-sql-server-management-studio-ssms?view =sql-server-ver15 Configure SQL Databases ● Open SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) ● Click Connect ○ For a local setup, log into SQL using default Windows Authentication for now. ○ If installing on a customer’s server, log into SQL using a predefined SQL user they should have provided, which needs either dbcreator server role or db_owner database role, or similar. The login needs full read/write/create/delete permissions to create databases and modify table data. ● Right click on the SQL server node itself, then under Properties > Security ○ Make sure SQL Server and Windows
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: they should have provided, which needs either dbcreator server role or db_owner database role, or similar. The login needs full read/write/create/delete permissions to create databases and modify table data. ● Right click on the SQL server node itself, then under Properties > Security ○ Make sure SQL Server and Windows Authentication mode is checked ● If installing for a local setup: ○ Build a progistics user - “progistics” username is common for many installations ■ Under Security > Logins > right click then New Login... ■ For the default local install you can just use: Sparq internal use only ● Username: progistics, password: progistics (ok for local, not for customer's server) ■ Uncheck Enforce password expiration ■ On Server Roles page check sysadmin (ok for local) ● Log out of SSMS and back in using your new progistics account. It should allow you to connect and access the Databases. (On a customer server, verify the account provided allows you to connect to the server.) ● Create these 5 DBs as named below (these are not added or predefined by the ConnectShip software which is installed later). All databases here are needed for a local setup. ● If installing on a customer server and the customer has an older ShipExec version, you will still need to create custom named databases for 2.x. ○ You will need to run a specified script after creating the first 3 databases below. (If the scripts aren’t available in a zip file in the Installation folder, you’ll need to copy them from the ConnectShip “Database Use and Distributed Installation” page: https://connectship.com/docs/A00028.aspx. Click on each SQL Server Transact-SQL Script link then copy the SQL script as needed.) ○ CS_Controller ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Controller). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Controller name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it, such as CS_Controller2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Controller Data Storage script (dbcontroller_60.sql) ○ CS_Transactions ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Trans). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Trans name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it such as CS_Trans2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Transaction Data Storage script (default_trans_62.sql) ○ CS_Configuration ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Config). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Config name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: ○ CS_Configuration ■ Note: If a customer has an older ShipExec version, they will already have this DB (i.e. CS_Config). If the customer has a new SQL server you can use the CS_Config name. If they have one main SQL server with older ShipExec DBs, create a new DB with the environment tag (_Dev) to differentiate it such as CS_Config2.x_Dev. ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ■ Select the DB, select New Query, then run the Server Component Configuration Data Storage (dbconfig_65.sql) ■ If this is a local install using only SQL Express: ● For this DB only, on the Files settings, on the LOG row edit the Autogrowth / Maxsize. Set Maximum File Size to 10,000 MB. ● On the Options settings set Auto Shrink to true ○ SE_AppDb ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) ○ SE_SoxDb ■ Set owner as progistics (or customer provided SQL user) Sparq internal use only Install UPS Specific Software Install ConnectShip and PMC ConnectShip and Progistics Management Console (PMC) are used to configure shippers and carriers used by ShipExec. PMC is installed along with ConnectShip, and is an integral part of ShipExec. It communicates with carriers during shipping and generates tracking numbers and labels. This section is only needed for a local install, or on a new customer's server. If a customer's server already has an older ShipExec version and PMC already installed, you can skip this section. ● For a customer install: ○ Connect to their 1.x Prod server and open PMC as an admin. Click Server Components and note or take a screenshot of the components listed to use in the new server setup. ● Log into the ConnectShip Members Area page then under Members, select Downloads. Click the ConnectShip Toolkit link, then on that page download the ConnectShip Toolkit 6.5 (not the Client-only), and install as an administrator. ● During the install, on the selection page under Server Components: ○ For a local setup, select just UPS (Consolidated) for now. ○ If it’s for a customer server, refer back to the Prod PMC server components notes or screenshot earlier and select those components in the list. This will make importing their PMC config later on less problematic. (Common customer server carriers might include Endicia, Global, LTL, UPS Freight, UPS (Consolidated), USPS (Endicia), etc.) ● Under Device Components, for Printers select PCL, Postscript, Windows, Zebra/Eltron ● When prompted to enter a Progistics user account password, if it’s for a local setup you can try progistics, but depending on the system you may need a simple but strong password, and be sure to note it ● When asked any questions regarding installing or updating SQL, always select NO ● If prompted to stop IIS during the install, select YES ● When prompted to download any available updates, select NO. Updates can be applied after install. ○ Note: Waiting on upgrading is also very important if you’re going to be connecting PMC to an existing
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: SQL, always select NO ● If prompted to stop IIS during the install, select YES ● When prompted to download any available updates, select NO. Updates can be applied after install. ○ Note: Waiting on upgrading is also very important if you’re going to be connecting PMC to an existing 1.x PMC version (more advanced setup), if a customer has 1.x installed. If you upgrade PMC it could be out of sync with their current 1.x version. ● When prompted to run maintenance, select NO. There will be post-installation Maintenance errors, select OK and ignore any that display. You will be installing updates at a later step once PMC is installed and configured. ● Once PMC is installed, open it as admin under Start > ConnectShip Progistics, and run the PMC (not the 32-bit version). Be sure to right click and run as an admin or you won’t be able to configure things. ● Note: It’s advised to change the start menu shortcut to always run PMC as admin under the properties. If PMC is not run as an admin, most options will be disabled and grayed out. Configure PMC Connections If a customer has an older ShipExec version already installed on the server, it’s recommended to use their existing ConnectShip Progistics Management Console (PMC) config, and you can skip this section. ● If you need to install PMC on a new customer’s server, access their current 1.x Dev server and export their current config with these steps: ○ Connect to their Dev server and run PMC as administrator Sparq internal use only ○ Enterprise > Import/Export Settings Data ○ Export Settings Data section, click Browse and select a path for the xml file ○ Export ○ Copy the xml file to the new Dev server, to be used later ● Open PMC as an administrator ● Load the license in PMC ○ Enterprise > Licensing ■ If you don’t already have the ConnectShip license from earlier steps, go to the ConnectShip site and when logged in, on the Members Home page, at the top there should be a License ID dropdown that lists all customer licenses you have access to. ■ For a local setup, use the license ID in the dropdown of CH89725, and if it’s not displayed, search for it in the dropdown. (That ID is general purpose assigned to Sparq only for our local development and testing, but not for customer use.) Copy the License Code and Password for use later. ■ If you’re installing PMC on a new customer’s server, look for the customer’s company name in the License dropdown in the top right. Select the customer name row to view their license ID and click Show beside the password, then copy both items. If they aren’t in the list or you don’t have access to the list, you’ll need to ask the customer for their PMC license login info. ■ Paste those values into the PMC License page then click Load. ■ Confirm it
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: click Show beside the password, then copy both items. If they aren’t in the list or you don’t have access to the list, you’ll need to ask the customer for their PMC license login info. ■ Paste those values into the PMC License page then click Load. ■ Confirm it Loaded Successfully ● Edit the PMC Controller Configuration ○ Enterprise > Controller Configuration Data ○ Select Location > SQL Server option ○ Add Connection string (CS_Controller database, progistics or customer login) ■ For a local setup, paste the connection string then modify as needed: ● server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;database=CS_Controller;user id=progistics;password=progistics; ■ For a customer’s setup, use a similar conn string as above to connect to the connectship databases you created earlier, such as ● server={customerSQLServer};database=CS_Controller2.x_Dev;user id={sql user};password={sql password}; ○ Note: using the Build button is optional since it’s used to build a connection string, and you already have a sample above. It might be useful if you will use windows auth to connect instead of a username/password. It will ask for the SQL Server and login, or can use windows auth. To use this feature: ■ Click Build… ■ Enter the SQL Server name, or click Refresh to view local or connected SQL Servers. Enter the SQL login or use windows auth. ■ Select the CS_Controller database ■ Click Save to create and update the connection string ○ Click Test, confirm it connects, then Save, then OK for confirmation ○ Copy the previous connection string to modify and use in the following steps ● Edit all Server Components’ Transactions/Configuration ○ Under Server Components, select each one to modify (ConnectShip UPS, etc) ■ Select the Transaction Data tab ■ Select Location > SQL Server option ● Add Connection string (CS_Transactions database, progistics or customer login) Sparq internal use only ● For a local setup, paste the connection string then modify: ○ server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;database=CS_Transactions;user id=progistics;password=progistics; ● For a customer’s server, paste the connection string then modify: ○ server={customerSQLServer};database=CS_Transactions2.x_Dev;user id={sql user};password={sql password}; ● Click Test and confirm success ● Do not click Save yet ■ Select the Configuration Data tab, select SQL server ■ Edit the string to use for the Configuration database, then click Test ■ For a local setup, paste the connection string then modify: ● server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;database=CS_Configuration;user id=progistics;password=progistics; ■ For a customer’s server, paste the connection string and modify to use, such as ● server={customerSQLServer};database=CS_Configuration2.x_Dev;user id={sql user};password={sql password}; ■ Click Save after you have added and tested both Configuration and Transaction connection strings. ■ If prompted to run maintenance, select Yes. Note the process for UPS alone can take a long time. ○ Repeat the above steps for all remaining Server components, such as USPS, etc ● If installing PMC on a customer’s server and you have their PMC exported settings xml file, do the following: ○ You can import the customer’s PMC settings under Enterprise > Import/Export Settings Data ○ Import Settings Data section > beside Import From > click Browse… and select the xml file to add the path, then Import ○ If
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: you have their PMC exported settings xml file, do the following: ○ You can import the customer’s PMC settings under Enterprise > Import/Export Settings Data ○ Import Settings Data section > beside Import From > click Browse… and select the xml file to add the path, then Import ○ If you receive errors about a server component not configured or missing, you will need to rerun the PMC setup and add the missing items: ■ Exit PMC ■ Rerun the PMC installer as an admin, like before. Click Next until you get to the components page ■ Expand the Server Components and select the missing component to install ■ Click Next and if prompted to stop IIS, select YES ■ Click Next and let things install ■ If prompted to install a local SQL Express instance, select NO ■ When prompted to download any available updates, select NO. Updates can be applied after install. ■ After the install, reopen PMC as an admin ■ Under Server Components, on any red components, be sure to add the Transaction and Configuration SQL connections as directed in above steps ■ Run maintenance on the component if prompted ■ Reimport the PMC config in the previous steps, to be sure Shippers are configured with the missing Carrier. You may receive errors about existing Shippers, which can be ignored. ■ The errors about Server Components (carriers) should be resolved. If not, seek help from team members. ○ If prompted to Run Maintenance, let it. That process can take a long time, perhaps 10 minutes or more as it updates the database. Sparq internal use only ● On the Server Components tab, check any components that are red and right click to Run Maintenance. Configure PMC Carriers / Shippers ● If installing on a customer's server: ○ If they already have an older ShipExec version and PMC installed, they will already have carriers and shippers defined, so you can skip this section. ○ If you were able to import their 1.x Dev PMC config in above steps, all their Carriers/Shippers and other info should already be defined, so you can skip this section. ● Open Progistics Management Console (PMC) as administrator ● (Most of the following steps are used when setting up PMC locally) ● On the ConnectShip Downloads page click on the Toolkit link to display the download/install page: https://connectship.com/members/InstallationGuides/Toolkit.aspx ● On the page, locate the Creating a Shipper section and follow its basic steps to set up a Shipper and Carrier, and Register them. ● You will need to add at least one shipper before installing ShipExec. Follow the guide to install and configure. The shipper will be used in your local ShipExec setup and testing later. ○ Note: When adding a Shipper you can enter bogus company info and complete all fields with test data. For Alias, create a short name in all caps with no spaces. Use a real City/State/Zip, but other fields don’t need to be authentic. ● Once you have
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: and testing later. ○ Note: When adding a Shipper you can enter bogus company info and complete all fields with test data. For Alias, create a short name in all caps with no spaces. Use a real City/State/Zip, but other fields don’t need to be authentic. ● Once you have added a Shipper, under Carrier Configuration > UPS Consolidated check Use Test Mode. Click Set to save. ● Add default Shipper account info and tracking start/end numbers. The start and end can be any values, but it’s recommended to use a 7 digit number (see example image) Sparq internal use only ● On the Contract Options tab set a default PLD Book Number 1 and 2 (see example image) ● Click Save Open Windows Services If installing on a customer's server with an older ShipExec version, if PMC is already installed and configured, you can skip this section, since Progistics services will already exist and be configured. ● Open windows Services. (For example using windows search, search for “services” then open the Services app.) ● Locate the Progistics services (see image below) ○ Check the Progistics AMP Service Startup type is set to Automatic and is running. If not, open the Properties and change the Startup type, then start it. This is the only service that needs to be running. ○ If the Amp service is not running: Sparq internal use only ■ Sometimes on a customer’s server, this service may not work under Network Service and need a custom domain login assigned with service account rights and allowed to start ○ The Automated Task Scheduler and Hub Client can be left as Automatic (Delayed Start) ○ The Remote Administration and XML Processor services can be left as Manual, since those are not often used ShipExec Installation ● Go to the ShipExec site and select Downloads: https://docs.shipexec.com/Downloads ● Change the version dropdown at the top of the page to 2.2 Update 6 or Update 4. Update 4 is a stable and non-buggy version. (Note - Do not use Current Version of 2.3 since it is not designed for an on-premise or local install, per UPS) ● Download all items and save them in a folder. It’s suggested to name the folder as the version number to organize and keep track of future versions, such as 2.2.22005.1. ● The download will create a zip for each program and requires a password to open. The password to unzip the files is PS1A$Ts0X Note: The following steps are only for installing ShipExec 2.2. For ShipExec 2.3, which is not recommended, please skip to that section below. ShipExec 2.2 ● For most of the installations you can take the defaults. You don’t need to run each as an admin, the installer will already do that. ○ Install Core Setup ○ This must be installed first ○ If you are installing on your local system, for the SMTP section it’s suggested to only set Port: 25, and leave the other values blank. (For a customer
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: run each as an admin, the installer will already do that. ○ Install Core Setup ○ This must be installed first ○ If you are installing on your local system, for the SMTP section it’s suggested to only set Port: 25, and leave the other values blank. (For a customer install that wants to send email from ShipExec when accounts are added or changed, and they have an SMTP server and config, these values can be updated later by rerunning the Core installer.) ○ As part of ShipExec Core, sending emails to a user when a password is created or changed in Mgt Studio, or when a user changes their password using the Forgot Password link in the Thin Client. These events are already handled in ShipExec. ○ Install Management Studio ■ Use default binding type of NetNamedPipe, do not select force HTTPS ○ Install ShipExec Thin Client Setup ■ Use default binding type of NetNamedPipe, do not select force HTTPS Sparq internal use only ○ Install CS Adapter ○ Install UPS Adapter Setup ○ Install UPS Tracking Adapter ○ (Osco Adapter - Not needed for a local setup. For a customer’s setup, this adapter is rarely used and not needed. Check with the customer to confirm if that product is used.) ○ Install Ship Exec Web Services ■ Use default binding type of NetNamedPipe, do not select force HTTPS ○ Install Scheduler Service Setup ○ Install ShipExec Client Support Setup ● On the desktop find the shortcut for ShipExec Configuration Tool ○ Right click > Properties > Advanced… > check Run as administrator > OK For ShipExec 2.2, skip to the section Configure ShipExec below ShipExec 2.3 1. Download or obtain the installation files for 2.3 2. Prior to starting the installation, install the sp.pfx certificate, selecting Computer/Local instead of Personal. Double click the certificate to start the process, enter password as the password. 3. Run the installers in the following order: a. ShipExec Core Setup 2.3 b. ShipExec CS Adapter Setup 2.3 c. ShipExec Management Studio Api Setup 2.3 i. For the thumbprint, enter 427a3d5b39df593b1d44b769d3697aedf00fd83f which is the thumbprint for the attached certificate (or the thumbprint for the certificate used) Sparq internal use only d. ShipExec Management Studio Setup 2.3 e. ShipExec Thin Client API Setup 2.3 i. For the thumbprint, enter 427a3d5b39df593b1d44b769d3697aedf00fd83f which is the thumbprint for the attached certificate (or the thumbprint for the certificate used) f. ShipExec Thin Client Setup 2.3 g. ShipExec UPS Adapter Setup 2.3 h. ShipExec Web Service Setup 2.3 i. ShipExec Scheduler Service Setup 2.3 Configure ShipExec ● Open the ShipExec Configuration Tool as admin ● Load License ○ Select Licensing ■ For your local setup: ● You will need to download the ASTLicense_17131.txt file in the shared google drive Installation folder and copy it to your local system to use later. This license was created for Sparq local ShipExec use only ● Note these items to use below… License ID: 17131, password: MaeQj%@o2h ■ For a customer setup, to obtain
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: download the ASTLicense_17131.txt file in the shared google drive Installation folder and copy it to your local system to use later. This license was created for Sparq local ShipExec use only ● Note these items to use below… License ID: 17131, password: MaeQj%@o2h ■ For a customer setup, to obtain the Sparq shipexec license or the license file info, use the following steps if you don’t already have their ShipExec License info: ● Navigate to the UPS Licensing site on the UPS VPN (http://svrt00010cfe.tus.ams1907.com/Customers) and search the customer by name, then select their name for a details page ● In the ShipExec Licenses section, copy the License ID and password ● Click the download icon to save the AST license file ○ Enter the customer License ID and password Sparq internal use only ○ Click Load License, find the AST license file then Open ○ The License should load successfully, and note the expiration date should indicate a year in the future, or not expired ○ Optionally, select the System Information option on the left pane and confirm the Licensee and ID are displayed ● (If installing on a customer's server, for the next few steps you can refer to the PMC steps above under the Server components for a basic SQL connection string.) ● If there are both Dev and Prod databases in their SQL server, be sure to set the conn string to the SE_AppDb_Dev or similar DB, depending on the server. ● AppDb tab ○ Connection string example: Server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;Database=SE_AppDb;user id=progistics;password=progistics; ○ If installing on a customer's server, the server name will be their SQL server, usually not localhost ○ Test, (click Test again for Save to enable), Save, Verify, Migrate ○ Note: if the Test doesn’t return successful, it’s possible the DB doesn’t have the correct permissions for a user or role ● SoxDb tab ○ Connection string: Server=localhost\SQLEXPRESS;Database=SE_SoxDb;user id=progistics;password=progistics; ○ If installing on a customer's server, the server name will be their SQL server, usually not localhost ○ Test, (click Test again for Save to enable), Save, Verify, Migrate ○ Note: if the Test doesn’t return successful, it’s possible the DB doesn’t have the correct permissions for a user or role ● Configuration tab ○ Update Adapters ○ Update ShipExec Information (only do this one time during the initial install) ● Restart the workstation or server (not needed but highly recommended) Update IIS Configuration (2.3 only) 1. Open IIS Manager 2. Copy the names of the ApplicationPools for the API website. By default, they should be: a. ShipExecManagementStudioApiAppPool b. ShipExecThinClientApiAppPool Sparq internal use only 3. Give the users access to the certificate a. Open a command prompt and enter “mmc” no quotes b. File -> Add/Remove SnapIn -> Highlight Certificates -> Click Add -> Select Computer account -> click Ok c. Expand the Certificates on the left d. Expand Personal e. Click on the Certificates folder f. Right-click on the certificate being used g. Select All Tasks h. Select Manage Private Keys i. Add the following
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: SnapIn -> Highlight Certificates -> Click Add -> Select Computer account -> click Ok c. Expand the Certificates on the left d. Expand Personal e. Click on the Certificates folder f. Right-click on the certificate being used g. Select All Tasks h. Select Manage Private Keys i. Add the following users i. IIS AppPool\ShipExecManagementStudioApiAppPool ii. IIS AppPool\ShipExecThinClientApiAppPool Sparq internal use only 4. Do the following steps for both Thin Client and Management Studio a. Navigate to the IIS directory that has the files for the Api site i. Open the web.config for editing 1. Change the JWEValidAudience value, replacing the machine name with localhost b. Navigate to the IIS directory that has the files for the Application site i. Open the web.config for editing 1. Change the ApiEndpointUrl value, replacing machine name with localhost 2. Change the ClientURL value, replacing machine name with localhost ii. Open config.json 1. Replace any instance of machine name with localhost 5. Restart the workstation or server (not needed but highly recommended) Configure Windows Services ● Using the windows search, search for “services” then open the Services app. ● Verify the ShipExec Client service is running (see image below). For a new install the WCF service will need to be started. The default startup type should be Automatic. If not, go into the Properties and change the Startup type, then start. Sparq internal use only ● The WCF service typically uses the Local System account. If the service fails to start and results in an error, ensure the license in the ShipExec Configuration Tool is valid and has a valid date range ● If the license is valid yet it fails to start, and you’re on a customer’s server, contact the customer’s IT dept for assistance. Sometimes the service might need its own domain login, or the login you’re using might need more permissions. It’s also possible the port for the WCF service is being used by another application or service. See steps below to use a different service login. ○ Note: You can skip these steps to use a different login if installing locally. ○ On a customer’s server, you may need to set the ShipExec services to use a dedicated admin account, as follows: ○ Find the ShipExec Client Service > Properties ■ Log On tab ■ Select This account, then Browse... ● Change to Entire Directory ● On Select User popup click Advanced… then Find Now ● Locate the admin user in the results, click OK ● The user should display in the object name box, click OK ● Enter the correct password ● On the Recovery tab, set First failure to Restart the service ● If the ShipExec Scheduler Service is not already running, try to start it. Depending on configuration it might not start, which is ok for now. The Scheduler service is used with Management Studio for scheduling custom file/data transfer jobs and isn’t required to be used. Securing ShipExec in IIS ● For a local setup, the default
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: not already running, try to start it. Depending on configuration it might not start, which is ok for now. The Scheduler service is used with Management Studio for scheduling custom file/data transfer jobs and isn’t required to be used. Securing ShipExec in IIS ● For a local setup, the default access for running ShipExec’s Thin Client, Management Studio and Web Service is via http. If you choose to use https to mimic how it might work on a customer's server, use the following steps. Otherwise you can skip this section. ● The ShipExecClientSupport installs a ShipExecClientService cert in Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager. That certificate is used by the server to communicate with the Client Support tool to access workstation machines and devices. You do not need to enable https for the Client Support tool to work. ● Note: On a customer’s server, using the ShipExecClientService cert for https is not allowed, per UPS. The customer will need to provide and install their own server SSL cert in IIS to be used with ShipExec. The type and strength of the cert is up to the customer and their organization’s IT standards. ● For a local setup, or if on a customer’s server and a custom SSL cert is installed, you can enable https with bindings and Require SSL settings. ● For initial testing of a local setup (or even on a customer’s server), you can leave the https settings unchecked only for testing the Thin Client and Management Studio. ● To enable https, open IIS and select the Default Web Site ○ Click Bindings… on the right ○ In the Site Bindings page click Add… Sparq internal use only ○ In the Type: dropdown select https, then in the SSL certificate dropdown select ShipExecClientService (only for your local install), or select the customer’s certificate, then OK ● Select ShipExecManagementStudio, then open SSL Settings ○ Check Require SSL and Client certificates: Accept, then Apply on the right ● For ShipExecThinClient, set Require SSL and Client certificates: Accept, then Apply on the right Confirm ShipExec is working ● Open ShipExec Management Studio on the desktop and confirm you get a login page. ○ If prompted with a private security warning, select Advanced then proceed ○ You will be prompted to create a ShipExec Administrator login, which is the “super admin” that’s for all companies on that server, used to add other users, and configure the company in Management Studio. Those steps are performed in the next section. ○ Create an admin-type named user with a secure password. You will use this admin account to log into Management Studio and manage all company config going forward. It should not be provided to the customer, so that our team has a login to connect and manage their company config. You will create a customer admin login in the next section. ShipExec company setup ShipExec Management Studio is an admin portal that allows you to create a company, manage customer company configurations, Thin Client settings,
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: customer, so that our team has a login to connect and manage their company config. You will create a customer admin login in the next section. ShipExec company setup ShipExec Management Studio is an admin portal that allows you to create a company, manage customer company configurations, Thin Client settings, and add/modify users for the company. Before using the Thin Client, you will need to add a new company (you won’t need to add an Enterprise or Site, just a Company). ● Add Company ○ The Name is the company name, and typically matches what is used in PMC for the customer (or your local test company) under a Shipper Company name ○ The Symbol is a short and unique version of the company name, in all caps ○ The License ID should be the ShipExec license number, entered in the ShipExec config to load the license (usually 5 digits) ● Edit the company to continue ● For a local setup: ○ Add a Shipper under Shippers. You only need the Name, Symbol and Company for now, and it should be like the shipper you added in PMC in earlier steps. Open PMC as admin if needed to find a shipper. ● For a customer’s server ○ You will need to add the Name, Symbol (abbreviation), and Company for each Shipper in PMC. Sparq internal use only ○ It’s recommended to set each shipper Name as “{company} ({symbol})” since that is what displays in the Thin Client, and should match how those shippers appear in their ShipExec 1.x Thin Client. ● Under Adapters, add the CSAdapter then edit it. If the adapter does not display after adding it, wait about 10 seconds, and if needed refresh the page, or click another tab then then click Adapters again. ○ Under Mappings, in the dropdowns select your local shipper and a matching shipper that resides in PMC, click Add Shipper Mapping, then repeat with other shippers if needed. ○ Click Save once all shippers have been mapped. You will need to run maintenance on the adapter after any changes. ● Under Printers, add a LP1 printer using the generic zebra driver and the UPS 4 x 8 1/4 stock and output to image (png), add a Document Config for UPS and the UPS Smart label, and a default Printer Config with the LP1 selected. These are needed later in order to print a label when shipping. ● Add a “default” Profile for all users, then edit it ○ Select the Shippers tab, and on the left select one or more Shippers, then click the >> button to move them to the Selected list on the right. ○ Add a Carrier (UPS), Services (UPS Ground and a few others), Countries (click Select All then >> to move), Package Types (all), Payment Terms (all), Hardware Config (Document config: default, Printer config: default) ○ Click Save. The profile settings you added are needed for the Thin Client to work. ● In the page banner on
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf

Text: (UPS Ground and a few others), Countries (click Select All then >> to move), Package Types (all), Payment Terms (all), Hardware Config (Document config: default, Printer config: default) ○ Click Save. The profile settings you added are needed for the Thin Client to work. ● In the page banner on the right, select User Manager to add new users: ○ Add User (for the Thin Client): ■ Set the Role to User, assign it to your test company, and set the default profile, and an Email to use as a user name (this does not need to be a valid user email). Under the Permissions tab Select All options, which affects available options in the Thin Client for that user. ■ Note: the password will be a temp one and you’ll be asked to change it on first login. ■ Click Save to add the user. If you receive a STMP error it can be ignored. ○ Add User (for customer to access Mgt Studio later) ■ Set the Role to Company Administrator, assign it to your company, and set the default profile. It’s advised to have the username include the word “admin” to easily differentiate it in the user list later. Set Permissions with Select All. ■ Note: the password will be a temp one and you’ll be asked to change it on first login. ● Open the Thin Client, which will take a while upon first launch. ● Log in with the User role account. After changing the password, select Shipping and Rating from the menu. You should see the Thin Client shipping page. If you have errors, contact a team member for help. There are more detailed setup steps you can follow along with, in a couple videos in the team shared drive Installation folder. Please review those videos to set up your local test company, create users, and complete the company setup. Alternately there is more info and examples in the ShipExec Documentation page under Management Studio and the Thin Client: https://docs.shipexec.com/Documentation Default ShipExec Server Business Rules Template Sparq internal use only ● Obtain the ShipExec Server Business Rules template from a team member. The template is a Visual Studio C# code template that contains ShipExec metadata, properties and interface methods. It will be used later on with a company in ShipExec to access custom Server Business Rules code. Sparq internal use only
Source: ShipExec 2.x and ConnectShip Installation and Configuration (most used).docx.pdf
